html: HyperText Markup Language, the standard markup language for documents designed to be displayed in a web browser.
css: Cascading Style Sheets, a style sheet language used for describing the presentation of a document.
js: JavaScript, a high-level, dynamically typed programming language used primarily on the World Wide Web.
electronegativity: strength/propensity to draw an electron towards oneself
monoclonal: (neoplasia) derived from a single mother cell; (antibody) made by the same B cell
